name: Dal Tadka
notes: Yellow lentil curry tempered with cumin, garlic, and ghee. Rich in plant protein and fiber. Common staple in Indian households.

name: Chicken Biryani
notes: Aromatic spiced rice dish with chicken. High in carbohydrates and moderate protein. Often paired with raita for probiotics.

name: Idli
notes: Steamed rice and lentil cakes. Low fat, easily digestible. Fermented batter improves gut health. Served with sambar and coconut chutney.

name: Paneer Tikka
notes: Grilled marinated cottage cheese cubes. Excellent high-protein vegetarian option. Good source of calcium. Popular for muscle-building diets.

name: Saag
notes: Slow-cooked leafy greens (spinach/mustard). Very low calorie, high in iron, vitamins A, C, K. Excellent for weight loss and micronutrient density.

name: Congee (Rice Porridge)
notes: Slow-cooked rice gruel, very gentle on digestion. Low calorie but can be enriched with egg or chicken. Popular recovery meal in China, Japan, Korea.

name: Edamame
notes: Young boiled soybeans. Complete plant protein with all essential amino acids. High in folate and fiber. Excellent pre/post-workout snack.

name: Salmon Sashimi
notes: Raw salmon slices rich in omega-3 fatty acids (EPA/DHA). Supports heart health, reduces inflammation. High-quality protein for muscle repair.

name: Tofu Stir Fry
notes: Firm tofu sautéed with vegetables in soy sauce. Versatile plant-based protein. Contains isoflavones with antioxidant properties.

name: Miso Soup
notes: Fermented soybean paste soup with tofu and seaweed. Rich in probiotics, supports gut microbiome. Very low calorie, excellent as meal accompaniment.

name: Greek Yogurt
notes: Strained yogurt with twice the protein of regular yogurt. Excellent source of probiotics and calcium. Ideal post-workout or breakfast option.

name: Hummus
notes: Blended chickpeas, tahini, lemon, garlic. Plant-based protein and fiber. Anti-inflammatory from olive oil. Great with vegetables or pita.

name: Grilled Octopus
notes: Lean seafood with exceptionally high protein and very low fat. Rich in vitamin B12, iron, selenium. Excellent for cutting phases.

name: Falafel
notes: Deep-fried chickpea patties. High in plant protein and fiber but calorie-dense when fried. Baking reduces fat content significantly.

name: Tabbouleh
notes: Parsley-based salad with bulgur wheat, tomato, lemon. Rich in vitamin K, C, and antioxidants. Very low calorie, high in fiber.

name: Black Beans and Rice
notes: Staple pairing across Latin America. Black beans provide complete amino acids when combined with rice. Excellent budget-friendly muscle fuel.

name: Ceviche
notes: Raw fish marinated in citrus juice. Very high protein, extremely low fat. Rich in vitamin C from lime. Great for fat-loss diets.

name: Churrasco (Grilled Beef)
notes: Brazilian-style grilled beef cuts. High-quality complete protein, rich in creatine, zinc, iron. Ideal for bulking and strength athletes.

name: Quinoa Bowl
notes: Andean grain that is a complete protein with all 9 amino acids. Gluten-free. High in manganese, phosphorus, folate. Excellent for vegetarians.

name: Arepas
notes: Colombian/Venezuelan cornmeal flatbreads. Gluten-free carb source. Customizable with protein fillings like chicken or beans for balanced meals.

name: Jollof Rice
notes: Tomato-based one-pot rice dish popular across West Africa. Good carbohydrate source. Served with chicken or fish for a complete meal.

name: Egusi Soup
notes: Ground melon seed soup with leafy greens. High in plant protein and healthy fats. Rich in vitamins A, C, iron. Served with fufu or rice.

name: Suya (Grilled Spiced Beef)
notes: Skewered beef rubbed with groundnut spice mix. Very high protein, lean cut with moderate fat. Popular street food; excellent post-workout meal.

name: Plantain (Boiled)
notes: Starchy fruit rich in potassium, vitamin C, B6. Natural complex carbohydrate. Boiled is healthier than fried. Good pre-workout energy source.

name: Akara (Bean Fritters)
notes: Deep-fried black-eyed pea fritters. Plant-based protein. High in folate and iron. Popular Nigerian breakfast. Baking reduces calorie count.
